summary: Implement BsonEncoder
description: Implement support for using {{Encodable}} structs/classes with our BSON library

summary: make Documents Equatable
Nil out pointer to bson_t when deinitializing a document
Display error message when an error is thrown
description: While we have string messages stored with several of the cases in  `enum MongoError`, these messages are not actually printed out when an error is thrown. We should figure out how to have errors with custom messages displayed.

summary: Add documentation for Document class
Implement Database.drop
description: This was accidentally omitted from the initial API sketch, but is needed for the alpha.

summary: Audit calls to UnsafeMutablePointer.allocate to make sure deallocate is always called after
description: As stated [here|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/unsafemutablepointer/2295026-allocate] we should always call "deallocate" when we're done with a pointer where we called allocate.

summary: Remove vendored libmongoc, link to system library
description: To aid in development, we have been compiling in a vendored copy of libmongoc and libbson. We should switch to linking dynamically against provided dylibs for these.

summary: Switch to Nimble for tests
Make Documents mutable/immutable depending on whether created with let/var
description: Currently, one can define a `Document` with `let` and then later on mutate it. By adding the `mutating` keyword to the subscript setter, we can make it more clear what is happening. This will likely require updating a number of places in the code where we rely on the current behavior.

summary: Add README to repository
Add "name" property to collection and database
description: We have easy access to these, and they may be useful to users.

summary: Convert benchmark results into a form that can be used for graphing on Evergreen
Change tests that rely on JSON files to work in Xcode, and document tests in README
description:
As we discovered from a [recent issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/48], it's not obvious that a mongod needs to be running for most of our tests. Additionally, the tests that rely on parsing JSON files don't work in Xcode. 

We should 
a) Add a section in the README about our tests
b) Fix the tests that use files so they can run in Xcode

summary: Make internal OpaquePointers to libmongoc types optional
description: In MongoClient, MongoDatabase, MongoCollection, MongoCursor, we initialize internal pointers to OpaquePointer(bitPattern: 1). In cases where initialization fails, this can lead to attempting to free the address 0x0000000000000001. We should instead make the pointers optionals with no initial value, so we don't try to free them if they aren't set to real addresses.

summary: Implement a custom RegularExpression struct
description:
This will allow us to make it Codable. Currently, we use an NSRegularExpression, which is not Codable and cannot be made to conform in an extension.

It should have a computed property that returns itself as an NSRegularExpression.

summary: Implement BSON Decoder
description: Implement support for using {{Decodable}} structs/classes with our BSON library

summary: support strict decoding of Document to a codable struct
Support JSON fallback in BSONDecoder.decode from Data
description:
Currently {{BSONDecoder}}s method for decoding Data assumes strict bson (expecting a wrapping object). It's possible for stitch to have bare JSON values, which are permitted in Extended JSON. We should add a new convenience method to {{BSONDecoder}} that: 
  - takes in JSON/extended JSON
  - attempts to create a {{Document}} from that data
  - if parsing into {{Document}} fails, fall back to using {{JSONDecoder}}

summary: Rewrite BsonEncoder to conform to Encoder protocol
description: We have a working BsonEncoder, but it will be more powerful if it conforms to the Encoder protocol and can work with all Swift Encodable types.

summary: Convert all options types to use the new BsonEncoder
Sort out Swift 4.0 vs Swift 4.1 issues
description:
As [~jason.flax] encountered, BsonDecoder is using compactMap in some copy-pasted code from the JSONDecoder, which is new in Swift 4.1. This was also pointed out by someone in a [GitHub issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/60] who wants to test the driver with IBM Z which only supports up to Swift 4.0. 

I have been sloppy about making sure I only use 4.0 stuff because I had 4.1 installed and didn't bother switching back, but I found a nice tool [swiftenv|https://swiftenv.fuller.li/en/latest/] to let us switch versions easily for the future. 

It seems like we probably want to keep supporting Swift 4.0 so we should resolve these issues. And standardize what version(s) we are testing (maybe we should set up travis or something?)

summary: Support encoding arrays at the top level
description: One should be able to encode an `[Codable]` and get back an `[Document]`

summary: Support building the driver on Linux
description:
We need a LinuxMain.swift file for the tests to run. Also, apparently Notification.Name requires an argument label - see this [issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/60]. 

Apparently once those issues are fixed, there is a segfault. 

We should resolve the issues that prevent the driver from building and then investigate the source of the segfault.

summary: upsertedId should be a `BsonValue?`, not `Any`
description: As pointed out by [~adam.chelminski@mongodb.com], UpdateResult.upsertedId is Any, but should really be a `BsonValue?`, since it will always be some valid BSON type if it exists. I believe this was a carry over from the CRUD spec where "Any" is used to mean "any BSON type".

summary: Refactor how ReadConcerns are encoded to options types
description: We've determined a better solution that involves making ReadConcern encodable and modifying the options before encoding to remove read concerns that the spec requires we omit. We should implement this, and then follow the same pattern for encoding options that support WriteConcern

summary: mongoc_init and mongoc_cleanup are never called
description:
We are presently not ever calling these functions, which the documentation for mongoc explicit states as a requirement.

The CXX driver requires the user to initialize a `mongocxx::instance` at the start of a program, which calls `libmongoc::init()`; the destructor for a  `mongocxx::instance` then calls `libmongoc::cleanup()`. 

We should implement this similarly, and maybe reference-count the mongoc instance in `MongoClient.deinit` to determine whether any other clients exist that point to it, and if not call cleanup.

summary: Move ReadConcernLevel type into ReadConcern
description: rather than having it as a separate type, let's move it into ReadConcern and rename it just Level so its full name is ReadConcern.Level.

summary: BSON Document should allow UnsafePointer where possible
description: BSON Document currently requires an UnsafeMutablePointer in various places where it is only reading BSON data. For flexibility, we accept an UnsafePointer.

summary: Send client info indicating we are in Swift driver
description: Right now it says we are using libmongoc. we should use mongoc_handshake_data_append to say we are using the swift driver

summary: Add support for "hint" and "CursorType" options to Collection API functions
description: We skipped these in the original implementation, but `find` supports a `cursorType` option and aggregate/count/find all support a `hint`.

summary: Support encoding/decoding untyped BsonValues
description: We should support encoding and decoding things that are only known to be BsonValues, since there are a limited number of BsonValue types and they are all Codable.

summary: Update MongoMobile to use new ABI v1 of MongoEmbedded
description: The new ABI has been released, and we need to update our builds accordingly

summary: Enable deleteOne-collation and deleteMany-collation crud tests
description: We were skipping some of the CRUD tests (those for deleteOne and deleteMany with collation) because of a bug in the C driver (CDRIVER-2527). This was fixed in v1.10 so we should update the test runner to run them now.

summary: FindOptions does not encode readConcern
description: [{{FindOptions.CodingKeys}} was removed|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/commit/e4c9487b976380ce61f1bd5fe81fa8a3a0a68367] in SWIFT-108 when the {{readConcern}} option was introduced, but it was missed when [{{FindOptions.CodingKeys}} was reintroduced|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/commit/5a654cf408d1edcf70dfbaecf704474459b29249] in SWIFT-63.

summary: Move BSON subtype into Binary
description: Similar to SWIFT-123, let's be consistent and nest types that are only used in conjunction with other types.

summary: WriteConcern CustomStringConvertible should return empty string on error
description:
I noticed that ReadConcern and WriteConcern have inconsistent implementations for CustomStringConvertible. If ReadConcern encoding fails, an empty string is returned. If WriteConcern encoding fails, a preconditionFailure is emitted. The Document type is also consistent with ReadConcern in returning an empty string.

While WriteConcern should never fail to encode as BSON, it should be consistent with the Document and ReadConcern implementations.

summary: Remove Document initializer that takes in a Dictionary
description:
if someone is creating a Document from a dictionary literal, they should do let doc: Document = [:] rather than let doc = Document([:]). the latter will not preserve key ordering and leads to double allocations.

if someone is trying to store String/BsonValue pairs to later put into a Document using this init, they could just use a Document to begin with. 

given the unexpected and non optimal behavior of this initializer, we should remove it

summary: Int should encode as Int32 or Int64 as needed
description:
The {{BsonValue.encode()}} implementation for Int requires that the integer's value falls within the range of a 32-bit signed integer. If Int contains a value outside of this range (possible based on the architecture), an error is raised that suggests Int64 be used instead. It should be possible for the driver to detect which BSON type is most appropriate.

I understand that when decoding from BSON, the driver converts Int32 and Int64 BSON types to a Swift Int and Int64, respectively. I assume Int was chosen for flexibility, as it jives with the [Swift documentation|https://docs.swift.org/swift-book/LanguageGuide/TheBasics.html]:

bq. Unless you need to work with a specific size of integer, always use Int for integer values in your code. This aids code consistency and interoperability.

Likewise, it's quite possible that applications will use an Int on a 64-bit platform to represent a 64-bit value and expect it to encode to BSON without error.

summary: 'swift build' - fails to download and compile dependencies
Hide bson_ts in BsonValue protocol
description: We already have an internal class wrapping a bson_t; we should make this type public and pass it around rather than the bson_t.

summary: MongoCollection::distinct() should return [BsonValue?]
description:
Per the [CRUD spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst], the {{distinct()}} helper should return {{Iterable<any>}} (see: [Terms|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/crud/crud.rst#terms]). In the context of the Swift driver, this should probably be an array of optional BsonValue types (i.e. {{[BsonValue?]}}).

Currently, it looks as if the method stuffs the [{{distinct}}|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/distinct/] command's response into a synthetic MongoCursor (see: [implementation|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/blob/28ca706546f713f52f2f87a7e103c31b041699a2/Sources/MongoSwift/MongoCollection%2BRead.swift#L77]). For reference, we can look to the [{{distinct}} example in the libmongoc docs|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/distinct-mapreduce.html#distinct-command].

summary: Clean up linting
description:
We have a lot of minor violations/"warnings", some of them for rules I'm not sure we actually care about using. Similarly, there are some swiftlint rules that might be useful to us but are not enabled by default.

We should decide what rules we want to disable / opt in to, and then fix all violations in the codebase.

We should also considering adding "swiftlint autocorrect" to "make".

We should have travis test this

summary: Hide bson_iter_t and refactor iterator code
description: We have a public type that wraps a bson_iter_t, DocumentIterator. We should pass this around instead, and try to condense all bson_iter_t initialization and traversal related code into that class.

summary: When UUID binary subtype is provided, validate that data is 16 bytes
Allow for null document IDs in inserted and upserted documents
description:
The InsertOneResult and InsertManyResult objects do not allow for null inserted IDs.

This may not be an issue for UpdateResult, as its {{upsertedId}} field is optional; however, the lack of a accessor for "upsertedCount" (introduced in CDRIVER-2704). may make it difficult for users to determine whether an upsert actually occurred.

summary: Swift driver not found in cocoa pods nor in Swift Package Manager
description:
When trying to install the swift driver, using the cocoa pods, an error is being thrown:

 *[!] Unable to find a specification for `MongoSwift(~> 0.0.2)`*

Same happens using the Swift Package Manager, it tells the package MongoSwift does not exist.

summary: Filter tests by current server version
description: We currently run all of our tests assuming the latest version of the server. A number of YAML/JSON tests contain mechanisms for determining whether a test should be run depending on server version. We need some way to conditionally skip tests based on server version in our tests.

summary: Add a way to get current server version in tests
description: In order to complete SWIFT-94, we will need a method to retrieve the current server version. We can do this by running the buildInfo command.

summary: Support map/filter/reduce-like operations on documents
description:
You can currently use "map" on a document since it's a sequence, this returns tuples with key/value pairs. I think we should make them feel as much like dictionaries as possible. Ideas to consider:

1) Change DocumentIterator.next to return labeled values, a la [DictionaryIterator.next|https://github.com/apple/swift/blob/8d386702aec53a6342a5020a6c235a7257833459/stdlib/public/core/Dictionary.swift#L4521]. 

2) Dictionary provides a `mapValues` method that maps over the values in a dictionary and modifies them. this would be nice to support, and we could add similar `filterValues` and `reduceValues` methods. I'm imagining an API like:
{code:java}
let doc = ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": "string", "d": nil]
let nonNil = doc.mapValues { $0 != nil } // ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": "string"]
let strings = doc.filterValues { $0 is String } // ["c": "string"]{code}
These would at least partially alleviate current concerns about documents being append-only since removing/modifying values would be easier

summary: Check that input to Decimal128 constructor is valid
description:
We currently just assume that the string is valid and won't throw until the user tries to encode it, but we should consider checking its validity upon initialization.

Alternatively, we could add a separate initializer that does the check, or an "isValid" method.

summary: Make Document SubSequence type a Document
description:
By default, the type of a SubSequence (the type returned from Sequence protocol methods such as dropFirst, dropLast, prefix, etc) is an AnySequence<Element>. Some Swift types as override this so you get back the same type. We should consider making Document Subsequences also Documents. This would allow you to do something like:

let doc1: Document = ["a": 1, "b": 2]

let doc2 = doc1.dropFirst() // ["b": 2]

Currently, doc2 would instead be an iterator that has a single tuple value, (key: "b", value: 2). 

The standard library actually implements "slice" types to return in these cases, which are views into the original storage. Implementing some kind of a Document view/slice type that doesn't actually copy storage could be a possible future optimization.

summary: "Returns nil if the write concern is unacknowledged" in various places is inaccurate
description: All of the collection write methods (in [MongoCollection+Write.swift|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/blob/master/Sources/MongoSwift/MongoCollection%2BWrite.swift]) say the method will return nil if the write concern is unacknowledged, but none of actually check if the write concern was acknowledged. This is also true for [bulkWrite|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/blob/master/Sources/MongoSwift/MongoCollection%2BBulkWrite.swift#L12], so that case could potentially be addressed in SWIFT-147 (cc [~jmikola]).

summary: Handle setting keys that already exist in a document
description: Right now, we don't check for a key's existence before appending it to the bson_t. This leads to unexpected results for users who are trying to overwrite values in a Document. We should check for a key's existence first and if it is already there, then create a new bson_t with the value replaced.

summary: Document.isEmpty returns the opposite
description: We return whether we successfully advanced the iterator, but if we were able to advance, it's not empty. So we need to negate the result.

summary: Rename mentions of Bson to BSON
description:
For people who are used to using {{JSONEncoder}} and {{JSONDecoder}}, it may be annoying that we lowercase the -son. I mess this up myself sometimes... We could even just do these as type aliases. 

Since this will cause inconsistency between the various BSON-related names we have in the codebase, we should rename those to use {{BSON}} as their naming convention as well, so that we don't fix this developer usability problem and add a new one, where we may mistakenly write {{BSON...}} instead of {{Bson...}} and vice-versa. Code relying on the lowercase version, {{bson}} will remain as is.

summary: Use new optimized document methods for key presence, count, etc. everywhere possible
description:
Since much of the driver was written, we've introduced new methods to Document that optimize common tasks:
* Document.hasKey
* Document.isEmpty
* Document.count

However, we aren't always using these. For example, in some places we use `Document.keys.contains`, but we should really now use `Document.hasKey`. Similarly, we should prefer `Document.isEmpty` over `Document.keys.isEmpty`, and `Document.count` over `Document.keys.count`.

summary: Update Cocoapods recipe to use frameworks
description: The mobile SDKs have been updated to generate frameworks for libbson, libmongoc and mongoe. The cocoapods for MongoSwift and MongoMobile should be updated to use these frameworks, instead of building custom frameworks themselves.

summary: Fix .travis.yml to fail whenever swiftlint has errors OR warnings
description:
Currently, our {{travis}} configuration runs {{swiftlint}} and uses its exit code to determine if there are any linter issues we should be concerned about. However, {{swiftlint}} will give a successful 0 return code even if there are linter issues. This is most likely because {{swiftlint}} does not consider all issues (or warnings) to be 'serious', and therefore not a failed linter check.

We should instead parse the output of {{swiftlint}} to see if we have any linter issues.

Something that {{swiftlint}} seems to do is output a single line at the end of its execution saying how many violations it found, e.g.:
{code}
Done linting! Found 16 violations, 0 serious in 31 files.
{code}

Parsing that line may be useful for fixing this.

*EDIT*: What may be simpler, actually, is to use the {{--strict}} option to {{swiftlint lint}}, which supposedly treats warnings as errors and should therefore cause a non-zero exit code, even if nothing 'serious' is found. This seems to do the trick, but like mentioned above, is also still triggered by third party packages. As long as we exclude them, we should be safe.

summary: Use brew to install a more recent version of swiftlint on Travis
description: Currently, our Travis configuration uses an out-of-date Swiftlint that is installed already. This causes us to get some error output from Swiftlint. Since we should have access to {{brew}}, we should use it to install a more recent version of Swiftlint on our Travis builds.

summary: Driver doesn't build in Swift 4.2
description:
On OSX 10.14, the driver will not build in Swift 4.2 with the following error output. Per users on this [GitHub issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/121#issuecomment-429968398], this is also an issue on Ubuntu and seems to be a Swift 4.2 issue rather than anything platform specific. 

I'm guessing it's something to do with how C names are getting imported to Swift but needs further investigation.

Note that we can ignore the weirdness about the version string, that is the usual strange error you get when there is a *different* libmongoc/libbson related error, which always disappears once you fix the actual error. Though I would like to get to the bottom of why that happens someday.
{code:java}usr/local/include/libbson-1.0/bson/bson-version.h:63:27: error: invalid suffix '.1' on floating constant
#define BSON_VERSION (1.13.1-dev)
                          ^
/Users/kaitlinmahar/mongo-swift-driver/Sources/MongoSwift/BSON/BSONValue.swift:468:41: error: use of unresolved identifier 'BSON_TYPE_CODE'
        if iter.currentType.rawValue == BSON_TYPE_CODE.rawValue {
                                        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Darwin.ACL_TYPE_CODA:1:12: note: did you mean 'ACL_TYPE_CODA'?
public var ACL_TYPE_CODA: acl_type_t { get }
           ^
bson.BSON_BYTE_ORDER:1:12: note: did you mean 'BSON_BYTE_ORDER'?
public var BSON_BYTE_ORDER: Int32 { get }
           ^
{code}

summary: Provide a way to compare equality of BSONValues
README link for "Things to install" in Development.md is broken
description:
The {{README}} link found in {{Development.md}} is broken. We should make it just point to the fragment of the GitHub repository pertaining to the installation (https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#first-install-the-mongodb-c-driver).

Currently, it points to https://mongodb.github.io/mongo-swift-driver/README.md#first-install-the-mongodb-c-driver, which gives a 404.

summary: Use setValue where possible rather than subscript setter
description: Some of our methods which throw are relying on the subscript setter. Since we now have a setValue method that throws, we should use this internally where possible to avoid unnecessary fatal errors.

summary: Use libbson overwrite methods for ObjectId, Timestamp, Datetime
description: libmongoc 1.13 will add support for overwriting these types in-place on a bson_t. We should use these to build on the work in SWIFT-196 that introduced overwriting other types (such as Int32, Int64) in-place.

summary: Add a throwing getValue method
description: Similar to setValue, we should add an internal getValue method that throws. This can be used in cases where the method retrieving a value from a document already throws and will make fatal errors less likely.

summary: Don't hard-code in port number for tests
description: Right now we hard-code localhost:27017 for the client in all of the tests. It would be nice if we could somehow accept a command line argument to {{make test}} that specifies the port to use.

summary: Create base class for functional tests
description: In [mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/97#discussion_r215792572], [~kaitlin.mahar] noted that {{getSpecsPath()}} is currently defined in an extension to XCTestCase. Rather than continue extending XCTestCase, we can introduce our own base class (e.g. MongoTestCase) that inherits XCTestCase and provides additional methods relevant to our own functional tests (e.g. getter for specs path, default database name).

summary: Make Document conform to Collection protocol
description:
The [Collection protocol|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/collection] inherits from the Sequence protocol, which Document conforms to as of SWIFT-195.

Conforming to Collection will require adding startIndex and endIndex properties (firstKey and lastKey), as well as an index(after: ) (basically nextKey) method. -We should likely just convert SWIFT-195's Document+Sequence extension to be Document+Collection and have it all live there.- Given the new understandings of what we will need to do too get true conformance to a {{Collection}} (w.r.t. runtime complexities), combining the two will probably lead to a big file, so splitting it may just be more manageable and organized.

The documentation notes that "Types that conform to {{Collection}} are expected to provide the {{startIndex}} and {{endIndex}} properties and subscript access to elements as O(1) operations. Types that are not able to guarantee this performance *must document the departure*, because many collection operations depend on O(1) subscripting performance for their own performance guarantees."

We could potentially provide the startIndex and endIndex properties in O(1) if we wanted to store them in the Document. We would be unable to do that for subscript access, but it seems like it's ok to just document that departure.

summary: Integrate xcpretty into test target
description:
[xcpretty|https://github.com/supermarin/xcpretty] makes Swift test output on the command line much more readable by adding color, spacing, symbols, snippets of failing code, etc, as shown in screenshot below.

although it is not mentioned on the repo, as described [here|https://forums.swift.org/t/swift-test-output-format/14702/13], it works with Swift test output if you run {{swift test 2>&1 | xcpretty}}. the 2>&1 is necessary as test output goes to stderr. 

we should add an option to make test (-p or something) that does the necessary redirect and piping.

we should complete SWIFT-103 in tandem with this, as that also involves modifying {{make test}}, and without SWIFT-103 our test output is still quite ugly because {{--filter}} (which we must use to exclude the benchmarks) places each matching test in a separate suite, leading to very lengthy output. 

!Screen Shot 2018-11-18 at 5.20.33 PM.png!

summary: Move benchmarks into a separate project
description: Right now they are just tests in the repo, which means that to run all non-benchmark tests (what we do by default) requires filtering. Filtered tests generate a lot more output - about 8 lines per test vs 2 - as each matching test is placed in its own suite, and this makes finding failures and reading the output very difficult. We should move the benchmarks into their own repo that depends on MongoSwift.

summary: Move Document.filter into a separate extension
Fix UnsafeMutableRawPointer! deprecation warnings in Swift 4.1+
description:
Building the driver in Swift 4.1+, one gets a few deprecation warnings of the form
{code:java}/Users/kaitlinmahar/mongo-swift-driver/Sources/MongoSwift/APM.swift:444:78: warning: using '!' in this location is deprecated and will be removed in a future release; consider changing this to '?' instead
                                             contextFunc: (OpaquePointer) -> UnsafeMutableRawPointer!
                                                                                                  ^                                         ~
                                                                                                                                             ?
{code}

We should fix these. It is possible that the code will still build in 4.0 with the ?, so we should try that approach before doing inline version checks.

summary: Fix deallocate deprecation warnings in Swift 4.1+
description:
When building the driver in Swift 4.1+, one gets several deprecation warnings like this:

{code:java}
/Users/kaitlinmahar/mongo-swift-driver/Sources/MongoSwift/BSON/Document.swift:322:22: warning: 'deallocate(capacity:)' is deprecated: Swift currently only supports freeing entire heap blocks, use deallocate() instead
            document.deallocate(capacity: 1)
{code}

I am pretty sure that the new way will not work in 4.0, but we should confirm that. It seems that Xcode 10 + swiftenv's 4.0 are not playing nicely together and I can no longer build the driver with 4.0 locally to test myself...

Assuming it doesn't work with 4.0, we can fix these warnings by using inline version checks, that use the new way for Swift 4.1+. See _BSONKeyedDecodingContainer.allKeys for an example of an inline version check.

It appears that all of these cases involve an UnsafeMutablePointer<UnsafePointer<Int8>?> or an UnsafeMutablePointer<UnsafePointer<bson_t>?>, so we can likely accomplish this using 1-2 shared methods that handle freeing pointer pointers, rather than adding the conditional in every place.

summary: Internalize Document.merge method
Improve handling of BSON nulls
description:
Right now, it is difficult for a user to determine whether a key is present and set to nil, or not in the document at all. They need to check if it is contained in the keys array, which is not very intuitive and also not very performant as it iterates the entire document.

We should consider how we can make it more obvious to users that there is a difference between a key not being present and a key being present and set to nil. 

One interesting option for this is using a double-optional (BsonValue??) as the Document value type rather than a BsonValue?. This would allow us to return a true nil in the case of a missing key, and an Optional(nil) in the case of a key that is present and set to nil. This is the behavior that a dictionary of [String: BsonValue?] has. Our current behavior more aligns with that of a [String: BsonValue]. See this [blog post|https://developer.apple.com/swift/blog/?id=12] for some discussion... A downside of that would be that users basically have to double unwrap values all the time. 

Alternatively, we can just better document how all this works, and add some optimized helper method(s) for checking if a key is present and set to nil. There is a bson_has_field method on bson_t that would be useful.

summary: ObjectId should expose timestamp
description:
According to [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/objectid.rst:]

Drivers SHOULD have an accessor method on an ObjectID class for obtaining the timestamp value.

summary: Loss of precision when initializing Date BSONValue from msSinceEpoch
description:
When using the {{msSinceEpoch}} initializer in our {{BSONValue}} extension of {{Date}}, some precision is lost. This initializer is used by the {{from}} method, so we can see the error when retrieving {{Date}} s from a document.

e.g.
{code:swift}let d: Document = ["a": Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.001)]
expect(d["a"] as? Date).to(equal(Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0.001)))
{code}
Fails with:
 {{expected to equal <1969-12-31 19:00:00.0010>, got <1969-12-31 19:00:00.0000>}}

There is also a loss of precision in the {{msSinceEpoch}} computed property, as it simply truncates as opposed to rounding.

summary: Replace pre-existing test code to use the new bsonEqual Nimble matcher
Move Document conformance to ExpressibleByDictionaryLiteral and ExpressibleByArrayLiteral into extensions
description:
For all other {{protocols Document}} conforms to, we declare conformance via {{extension}}. We should do the same for these.

the reason we didn't initially was that classes must have protocol-required initializers in their declarations, and {{Document}} used to be a class. 

now that it is a {{struct}}, it's cleaner and more consistent to move these out to extensions.

summary: Move internal Document methods to a separate extension
description:
The Document struct declaration is getting to be quite lengthy (~350 lines), and contains a number of internal methods.

For readability/organizational purposes, we should pull out all the private/internal stuff into an extension, leaving just the public API and properties in the definition.

summary: Make test database name accessible from the base class
description:
Currently, we have hardcoded repetition of the test database name scattered throughout our testing code. This is pretty bad and annoying if we ever decide to use a different database name or if we want to do some functionality changes that involve doing something similar to generating a database name.

We should add a method to the testing base class introduced by SWIFT-207 that returns the testing database name when called.

We should then change these instances of hardcoded repetition in the codebase to call this new method.

summary: Add @dynamicMemberLookup to Document
description:
Swift 4.2 introduces the [@dynamicMemberLookup attribute|https://github.com/apple/swift-evolution/blob/master/proposals/0195-dynamic-member-lookup.md]. Basically, this is just syntactic sugar, allowing you to use dot notation to look up arbitrary properties on a type.

A natural place for us to use this is on documents. This will allow users to do things like the following:
{code:java}var doc: Document = ["a": 1, "b": 2]
print(doc.a) // prints 1
print(doc.x) // prints nil
print(doc.b) // prints 2{code}
Implementing this will require adding the attribute to Document, and adding a new subscript(dynamicMember: ) method that behaves identically to our existing subscript getter.

summary: Update podspecs to use frameworks provided by Platforms
Add a way to provide a default value when looking up a key in a Document
description:
In many languages (including Swift) there is a way to provide a default value to use for a key that isn't present in a dictionary. 

The Swift-y way incorporates the value into the subscript getter:
{code:swift}let value = doc["x", default: 0]{code}
See documentation [here|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/dictionary/2894528-subscript].

We should consider supporting this on Documents. 

Note we will need to think carefully about behavior when there are null values in documents.

summary: bsonEquals does not work for arrays of different length
description:
This test fails:

```

func testBsonArrays() {
 let b0: [BSONValue] = [1, 2]
 let b1: [BSONValue] = [1, 2, 3]
 XCTAssertFalse(bsonEquals(b0, b1))
 }```

summary: Allow .uuidDecodingStrategy and .dateDecodingStrategy to be set for BSONDecoder
Use generated collection names for tests
description:
As discussed in [mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/97#discussion_r215792572], we should use generated names for test collections. This would minimize the chance of different tests using the same collection and inadvertently running with pre-existing data. It may also allow us to leave data behind for failed tests if we like (PHPLIB does this).

The database name can be constant (where possible) throughout the test suite.

summary: Allow .uuidEncodingStrategy and .dateEncodingStrategy to be set on BSONEncoder
description: Same principal as SWIFT-281, but for the encoder.

summary: bsonEquals should be public
description:
Implementing SWIFT-217, I caught that {{bsonEquals}} does not have an access level specified and it is therefore {{internal}}. 

I've corrected it in that PR to appease the linter, but I'm opening a separate ticket to ensure that we get this change in for the 0.0.8 release, regardless of where that PR is when we tag the release.

While the newly added {{explicit_acl}} rule will probably catch most of these cases, I believe this further supports the suggestion made in SWIFT-292 that we move toward separately testing private and public API.

summary: BSONNull should be Equatable
description: The rest of our public BSONValue types are, so we should be consistent.

summary: retryWrites is not set on client
description:
Currently, we do not set the retryWrites option in the {{MongoClient}} initializer, regardless of whether the user specifies the option or not.

Currently there is no way to set it directly on the client in libmongoc, so we will have to set it on the URI before creating the {{mongoc_client_t}} .

summary: Unify decoding and encoding strategies
description:
Right now, the encoding strategies are defined as {{.deferTo}} ,whereas the decoding strategies are defined as {{.deferredTo}}. We should refactor the encoding strategies to be in line with both the decoding ones and with {{JSONEncoder}} .

Also, the documentation should be changed to use similar wording.

summary: MongoMobile should cleanup only when instructed
description: Currently, MongoMobile cleans up every time [{{MongoMobile.close()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/swift-mongo-mobile/blob/b0893b682a5305bceb51305d6b11818f2c1aa5f6/Sources/MongoMobile/MongoMobile.swift#L105] is called. This means you cannot close and reopen an instance, which effectively means that you can only use one db path per session. This is not expected behaviour.

summary: AnyBSONValue should conform to Hashable
description:
{{BSONValue}} does not currently conform to {{Hashable}}, but it should, for the same reason it should conform to {{Equatable}}, since all {{BSONValue}}'s are themselves {{Hashable}}.

[~jason.flax] provided a sketch for what the implementation of this may look like:
{code:java}
private func _hash(into hasher: inout Hasher, bsonValue: BSONValue?) {
    switch (bsonValue) {
    case (let value as Int):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Int32):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Int64):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Double):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Decimal128):
        hasher.combine(value.data)
    case (let value as Bool):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as String):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as RegularExpression):
        hasher.combine(value.options)
        hasher.combine(value.pattern)
    case (let value as Timestamp):
        hasher.combine(value.timestamp)
    case (let value as Date):
        hasher.combine(value.timeIntervalSince1970)
    case (_ as MinKey):
        hasher.combine(1)
    case (_ as MaxKey):
        hasher.combine(1)
    case (let value as ObjectId):
        hasher.combine(value.description)
    case (let value as CodeWithScope):
        hasher.combine(value.code)
        _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: value.scope)
    case (let value as Binary):
        hasher.combine(value.data)
    case (let value as Document):
        hasher.combine(value.canonicalExtendedJSON)
    case (let value as [BSONValue?]): // TODO: SWIFT-242
        return value.forEach { _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: $0!) }
    default: break
    }
}

extension BSONValue {
    func hash(into hasher: inout Hasher) {
        _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: self)
    }
}
{code}

It is worth noting that unlike SWIFT-254, this does (should) _not_ depend on SWIFT-215 to be implemented.

summary: When a document is missing an `_id`, prepend one to a copy of the document before sending to server
description: To be in line with other drivers we should prepend the _id field before sending to the server.

summary: Do not modify input documents missing _ids in insertOne and insertMany
Add a .dataEncodingStrategy and .dataDecodingStrategy to support storing Data in BSON binary format
description:
The {{encode(to: encoder Encoder)}} implementation for {{Data}} writes a {{[UInt8]}} to the provided container. The {{init(from decoder: Decoder)}} implementation attempts to decode a {{[UInt8]}} from a container. 

Since {{BSON}} does not support integer types besides {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, we ended up writing logic (see the {{CodableNumber}} protocol) that will store {{UInt8s}} as {{Int32}} s in a document, and that will allow decoding any {{Int32}} or {{Int64}} that can fit into a {{UInt8}} as a {{UInt8}}.

All of that is to say that round-tripping a struct containing {{Data}} with the encoder and decoder works, but it is inefficient, as each byte ends up taking up 4 bytes in the document!

Our {{Binary}} type is initializable from {{Data}}, so for optimal encoding users should wrap their {{Data}} in a {{Binary}} before its encoded, and then request a {{Binary}} from the decoder.

However, users do not always have control over the types they are using, so it would be nice to allow setting a {{.dataEncodingStrategy}} of {{.binary}} that automatically wraps the {{Data}} in a {{Binary}}. Similarly, {{.dataDecodingStrategy = .binary}} would look for a {{Binary}} in the document being decoded from.

summary: Consider whether to be using fatalErrors vs precondition/preconditionFailures
description:
[precondition|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1540960-precondition] and [preconditionFailure|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1539374-preconditionfailure] are not evaluated in unchecked builds ( -Ouncheck), but [fatalError|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1538698-fatalerror]s are.

we pretty much just use precondition/preconditionFailure everywhere. Swift std lib uses a mix of both.

we should further research this and come up with a rule of thumb as to whether to use fatalError or precondition in various places, and correct any existing violations. 

blog posts that seem promising for learning more:
 * [https://agostini.tech/2017/10/01/assert-precondition-and-fatal-error-in-swift/]
 * [https://blog.krzyzanowskim.com/2015/03/09/swift-asserts-the-missing-manual/] 
 * [https://www.swiftbysundell.com/posts/picking-the-right-way-of-failing-in-swift]

summary: Introduce new errors and error types
description:
Add definitions for the new error enums and error details structs.

The errors are as follows:

*ServerError*
 commandError(code, message)
 * Thrown when commands that experience errors on the server side that prevent execution
 * From the Write Commands spec: “a command failure, which indicates the command itself did not complete successfully. Example command failures include failure to authorize, failure to parse, operation aborted by user, and unexpected errors during execution (these should be very rare).”

writeError(write: WriteErrorDetails?, wc: WriteConcernErrorDetails?)
 * Thrown from write commands that experience errors on the server side
 
bulkWriteError(writeErrors: [BulkWriteErrorDetails]?, writeConcernError: WriteConcernErrorDetails?, result: BulkWriteResult)
 * Thrown from Bulk write operations that experience errors on the server side

*UserError*
 logicError(message)
 * Thrown when user uses the driver in an improper way

invalidArgument(message)
 * Thrown when receiving an error with code COMMAND_INVALID_ARG from a mongoc call or when we can determine invalid arguments at the Swift level

*RuntimeError*
 internalError(message)
 * Thrown when something is null when it shouldn’t be, mongoc has an internal error, can’t understand server response
 * Should generally be indicative of a bug, either in Swift, c driver, or mongod

connectionError(message)
 * Any connection related failures (MONGOC_ERROR_STREAM domain)

authenticationError(message)
 * Any auth related failures (MONGOC_ERROR_CLIENT_AUTHENTICATE)

Also, define a function that will return an appropriate error given a libmongoc error code and domain.

summary: Update CRUD API errors to match the new hierarchy
description:
We don't really follow the CRUD API's specification of error types and all the values that should be included. we should audit these errors and make sure we include the necessary info.

we should also think about ways we can incorporate our BsonDecoder to convert reply documents into errors.

Using the new error hierarchy, we should:
* Throw command errors in the event of actual commandErrors
* Throw appropriate [Bulk]WriteErrors for write errors, complete with results and all errors contained therein
* Remove InsertManyError, throw a BulkWriteError instead.

summary: Convert InsertManyResult to BulkWriteResult when insertMany throws BulkWriteError
description: InsertMany is implemented as a bulk write and is meant to throw a BulkWriteError as per the crud spec. We'll need to convert the InsertManyResult to a BulkWriteResult so it can be included in a BulkWriteError thrown by {{insertMany()}}.

summary: Convert invalidCollection, invalidClient, invalidUri, and invalidResponse errors to internalError or fatalError
description: These "invalidX" errors typically catch when libmongoc returns null (memory allocation failure) or some other internal error occurs. If one of these errors is thrown where it can be recovered from nicely, thrown an {{internalError}}. Otherwise, use a {{fatalError}} .

summary: Convert invalidCursor errors to commandErrors, logicErrors and invalidArgument errors
description:
{{invalidCursor}} is currently used as a catch-all for errors related to cursors. This includes errors in the creation of the cursor and errors that occur in calls to getMore. 

We should distinguish between these by appropriately throwing either {{commandErrors}}, {{logicErrors}}, or {{invalidArgument}} errors.

summary: Convert bsonParseError to internalError
description:
Currently, everywhere bsonParseError is thrown, an internal problem has occurred within libmongoc (e.g. memory allocation failures)

Replace all instances where this is thrown with the new {{internalError}}.

summary: Convert readPreferenceError to invalid argument or logic errors
description:
Currently we throw readPreferenceErrors whenever something related to readPreferences has a problem. We should be more specific and instead throw errors that detail immediately what is wrong.

e.g.
an out of range maxStalenessSeconds is an invalidArgumentError
setting tagSets when the mode is primary is a logicError

summary: Convert bsonEncodeError, bsonDecodeError, typeError to new error types
description:
In our encoders and decoders, we currently throw many different types of errors, some of which are redundant or incorrect.

We should update them to throw encodingError, decodingError, logicErrors, internalErrors, or invalidArgumentErrors as necessary.

Also, when encoding/decoding directly to/from a document without the use of {{BSONEncoder}} / {{BSONDecoder}} (i.e. through {{BSONValue}} conformance methods ), we should be throwing appropriate errors. In particular, we should be throwing {{.logicError}} s when the user attempts to retrieve a value from a {{DocumentIterator}} that doesn't match the current type of the iterator.

summary: Remove old errors
description:
At this point, (almost) all of the old errors should be unused. Remove the definition of {{MongoError}} and cleanup any references to the remaining ones in code.

File follow up tickets as necessary for any lingering issues.

summary: Move RegularExpression.nsRegularExpression logic into a new NSRegularExpression initializer
description:
Because {{NSRegularExpression}} doesn't quite align with a BSON regular expression, we chose to write our own {{RegularExpression}} type and provide facilities on it for converting to an from {{NSRegularExpression}} (with some lossiness of options). 

The {{NSRegularExpression}} -> {{RegularExpression}} conversion is implemented via an initializer: {{RegularExpression.init(from regex: NSRegularExpression)}}. 

The opposite conversion is implemented as a computed property on {{RegularExpression}}: {{var nsRegularExpression: NSRegularExpression}}. 

I think it would be more consistent/symmetric to instead add a new convenience initializer to {{NSRegularExpression}} via extension that takes in a {{RegularExpression}}.

summary: Don't require connectionString label in MongoClient initializer
Correctly encode strings with multi-byte UTF8 characters
description:
All C driver methods that take in strings also take in an Int32 corresponding to the string's length.
In all these cases, we use the {{String.count}} property to get the length.
However, to handle multi-byte UTF8 characters, we should actually be using {{String.utf8.count}}.

Related reading: 
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/string
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/string/utf8view

summary: Allow a user logger to be set in MongoMobile
description: This should use mongo_embedded_v1_log_callback: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blob/master/src/mongo/embedded/mongo_embedded/mongo_embedded.h#L366

summary: Throw an error when a non-BSONEncoder is used in encode(to:) for all BSONValue types that we own
description: For BSONValue types that we created (not Swift types we extend), we should throw errors when you try to encode them using a non-BSON encoder, as the synthesized implementations are often incorrect.

summary: Throw an error when non-BSONDecoder is used in init(from: decoder) for BSONValue types
description: For BSONValue types that we created (not Swift types we extend), we should throw errors when you try to decode them using a non-BSON decoder, as the synthesized implementations are often incorrect.

summary: Improve error reporting in mobile library
description:
Before we do a 1.0 release of swift-mongo-mobile, we should improve our error reporting.

Things to consider:
 * We have a couple of {{MongoMobileError}} cases that are never used.
 * Another couple cases have empty closures as associated values, ie they are defined as something like {{invalidClient()}}. It is unnecessary to do that. These cases should just be defined as {{invalidClient}} with no parentheses. 
 * In cases where the embedded library was already initialized or cleaned up (we can tell this by whether the shared {{libraryInstance}} property is non-nil), we could short-circuit and provide a more specific error, like {{MongoMobileError.logicError}} or something like that. This would be consistent with the driver and allow distinguishing between user error (i.e. repeated calling {{initialize}} and {{close}}) and any other cases that can cause the embedded lib init and cleanup methods to fail. 
 * We have two error types, {{MongoMobileError}} and {{MongoEmbeddedV1Error}}. Arguably, the second could just be a case of the first, rather than its own type. 

We should also clearly document what types of errors each method can throw.

summary: Investigate XCode 10.2 Issues
Round trip Symbols, DBPointers, and Undefineds correctly
description:
When we encounter the deprecated BSON types Symbol and DBPointer in documents, we return them as Strings and Documents, respectively. So if you round trip these types you do not get the same result out. 

When we encounter a BSON undefined, we just error, which is even worse. This has been a problem for the Stitch team as they decode server responses that may contain an undefined.

We should expose all of these types in the public API, but make their initializers internal so that users cannot create new ones themselves in the driver, and can only read them out of existing documents.

summary: Test round-tripping of BSON types with native representations using the BSON corpus data
description:
The BSON corpus test runner is written to follow the specification instructions for a language with no native intermediate representation of BSON. While it is true we do not have a native representation of a document, since we just wrap {{bson_t}} s which are already stored in BSON format, we *do* have native representations of all the other BSON types.

We should implement the round trip tests for languages with native representations by adding assertions that do something like: 
 1. Parse a document from the raw data
 2. Recursively convert the document to a {{[String: BSONValue]}}. This will convert every stored value to a native Swift type. (I don't think we can use the decoder here, as it will not recursively parse nested documents into maps.)
 3. Recursively convert the {{[String: BSONValue]}} back into a document. This will encode every stored value back to BSON. 
 4. Check that the new document matches the input

(Note: may need to do something special to preserve key order since dictionaries don't guarantee it)

This would allow us to catch bugs like SWIFT-351 for example – there are [BSON corpus cases for multi-byte UTF8|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/8d1187be4094418ed130e740c067b8de20b65d92/source/bson-corpus/tests/string.json#L22], and converting the stored strings to Swift strings and then putting them back into documents would have shown us that we cut off the end of string.

summary: Improve error message for type mismatches when decoding driver introduced BSON types
Make bson pointer access explicit
description: We pass references to {{bson_t}} and {{bson_iter_t}} liberally in the codebase, leading to some confusion about whether the pointers are being used in a mutable way or not. Additionally, by making this more explicit we can create easy paths for supporting a change to Swift 5 where these types are imported as {{OpaquePointer}}

summary: Include bsonEquals as part of BSONValue protocol
Provide an internal accessor for `bson_t`s length
description: In Swift 5 {{bson_t}} is imported as an {{OpaquePointer}}, meaning that we will lose type information from within Swift. As a result, we no longer have a way to determine the length of the {{bson_t}}. We can provide a method in our {{swift-bson}} package to provide this functionality.

summary: TopologyDescription initializer doesn't clean up server descriptions array
description: docs for [mongoc_topology_description_get_servers|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/mongoc_topology_description_get_servers.html] say we need to call  {{mongoc_server_descriptions_destroy_all}}, but we don't.

summary: Ensure all bson_ts are correctly cleaned up
description:
An issue reported on GitHub (linked below) led us to discover a few different places in the driver where we call `bson_new_from_x` (x being data or json), and then pass the result into {{Document(fromPointer)}} (which does a {{bson_copy}}), but never clean up the result. 
We should ensure that every time we call these methods we clean up their results properly.

We can either just add {{bson_destroy}} s, or a more performant option would be to add a new {{DocumentStorage}} initializer that "steals" the {{bson_t}} rather than copying its memory.

summary: Provide better consistency around Int usage with Documents
description:
the {{Int}} type in Swift is tricky to handle correctly, because its bitwidth is machine-dependent. But it is also used far more often than {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, so it's important that the behavior is intuitive to users.

The current behavior is as follows:

Putting into documents:
 * If the {{Int}} will fit in an {{Int32}}, its {{bsonType}} is considered {{.int32}} and it will be encoded to the document as an {{Int32}}. 
 * Otherwise, its {{bsonType}} is {{.int64}} and it will be encoded to the document as an {{Int64}}.

Reading out of documents:
 * Any value encoded as an {{Int32}} will be returned as an {{Int}} 
 * Any value encoded as an {{Int64}} will be returned as an {{Int64}} 

There are some problems with this behavior. As [~jmikola] pointed out: consider a user who stores an {{Int}}, which could be anything in {{[Int.min, Int.max]}}. 

 
{code:java}let a = Int(Int64.max)
let doc: Document = ["a": a]
print(a) // 9223372036854775807
print(doc["a"] as? Int) // nil
print(doc["a"] as? Int64) // Optional(9223372036854775807)

let b = Int(Int32.max)
let doc: Document = ["b": b] 
print(b) // 2147483647 
print(doc["b"] as? Int) // Optional(2147483647)
print(doc["b"] as? Int64) // nil{code}
When the user retrieves the value for that key, they will get back a {{BsonValue?}}. If the user then wants to cast the value to a type, they must either know ahead of time how big the value is, or alternatively switch on the {{bsonType}} property, or just try both {{Int}} and {{Int64}} and see which works.

It's not clear what the best solution is, but we should aim for something with consistent/predictable behavior that does not require the user having prior knowledge of the value stored. 

The way MongoKitten handles this is by always encoding {{Int}} as {{Int64}}.

Inspired by that approach, one possibility is:
 * Always convert an {{Int}} to an {{Int64}} and then write it to the doc.
 * When reading an integer value from a doc, return {{Int32}} or {{Int64}}. whatever it was encoded as
 * Upsides:
 ** predictable behavior. {{Int32}} and {{Int64}} preserve their types when round-tripped, and {{Int}} always becomes {{Int64}}.
 * Downsides:
 ** on a 64-bit platform, half of the {{Int}} range can be represented with just 32 bits. on a 32-bit platform, the entire {{Int}} range can be represented with just 32 bits. so this isn't particularly efficient in those cases space-wise. 
 ** {{Int}} is used a lot more commonly throughout Swift libraries than {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, so users are going to have to do a lot of casting.

summary: Store bson_oid_t in ObjectId rather than creating one each time we need it
description:
Currently, ObjectId stores its data as a String. When we need to interact with libmongoc, we use this to initialize a bson_oid_t. 

this is rather costly and there is no clear reason why we aren't just keeping around and reusing one bson_oid_t the whole time.

we should do that instead, and expose the String version of the ObjectID, along with the timestamp, as computed properties.

summary: Enable evergreen builds on macos
description: {{swiftenv}} currently requires sudo privileges, and wants to install versions of swift to the root ({{ / }}) directory, which we are not allowed to do on our evergreen macos hosts. A patch to {{swiftenv}} could allow for this, but until then we will have to keep testing our builds on travis.

summary: Modify Vapor example to show using one client per eventloop
description: Since {{MongoCollection}} is not thread safe, we should edit the example so its clear to users they need to use a different client in each thread.

summary: expireAfter index option is not correctly encoded
Document.count should not be publicly settable
description: We should mark it {{internal(set)}} so that users can't update it themselves.

summary: Resync retryable writes tests
description: See DRIVERS-476 for details.

summary: Initial aggregate command for a change stream should not be retried
description: See DRIVERS-474 for details.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description: See DRIVERS-506 for details.

summary: Remove cursor `snapshot` option
description: See DRIVERS-454 for details.

summary: Implement operations layer for driver
description: Convert all calls to {{libmongoc}} to use operations, allowing us to run these calls on a thread pool, and ensure thread safety of those calls.

summary: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests
description: See DRIVERS-505 for details.

summary: Resync transactions spec tests
description: See DRIVERS-500 for details.

summary: Make properties of WriteModel implementations public
description:
Right now, there is no way to inspect the properties of a {{WriteModel}} once constructed (e.g. {{UpdateOneModel}}, {{ReplaceOneModel}}, etc.). This is problematic for one of our use cases as we have both local collections represented as {{MongoCollection}} which DOES support bulk writes, and remote collections managed by Stitch that DO NOT support bulk writes. We'd like to use the same API to defer writes to these collections as we do in Java by using {{WriteModel}} implementations and simply unwrapping the filter/update for the non-bulk use case, but we cannot because the properties (filter, update, options) are not exposed.

Per discussion with [~kaitlin.mahar], this ticket proposes making them public.

summary: Handle MongoSwift name conflicts
description:
In Swift, it is possible and common that two modules may define types with conflicting names. If a user imports both of them in the same file, they can disambiguate which type they mean by prefixing it with the module name. For example, if ModuleA and ModuleB both have a type {{Foo}}, the user can refer to a specific one with {{ModuleA.Foo}} or {{ModuleB.Foo}}. 

The driver defines a class called {{MongoSwift}} that handles libmongoc initialization and cleanup via two methods, {{MongoSwift.initialize}} and {{MongoSwift.cleanup}}. This class name is problematic because it makes it difficult for users to disambiguate our types as described above. {{MongoSwift.Foo}} will look for a member of the class named {{Foo}}, rather than a type in the library named {{Foo}}. There are some workarounds for this, as described [here|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/37892621/how-can-i-disambiguate-a-type-and-a-module-with-the-same-name]: users can add typealiases in separate files, or use a special import syntax for the type in question.

We have a few options for dealing with this: 

1) Do nothing. Users can use the solutions in the post linked above to work around this if/when they encounter a naming conflict. IMO, not optimal because the workarounds are non-obvious, and the compiler error you get trying to do this the normal way is confusing if you don't realize we have a class called {{MongoSwift}}. 

2) Move the {{initialize}} and {{cleanup}} functions to be free-floating and get rid of the class. This won't break anyone's existing code, as these functions would still be named {{MongoSwift.initialize}} and {{MongoSwift.cleanup}} (the MongoSwift prefix would just be unnecessary in most cases.) 

3) Move he {{initialize}} and {{cleanup}} functions to be free-floating and rename them more descriptively (e.g. {{initializeMongoSwift}}), and get rid of the class. 

4) Rename the class to something else, e.g. {{MongoSwiftDriver}}.

summary: Refactor Database/Collection initializers to take in options, instead of libmongoc types
description: As pointed out by [~matt.broadstone] in this [comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/208#discussion_r269582972], instead of configuring the libmongoc type then passing it to the Database/Collection initializer, we should just pass the options straight to the object being initialized.

summary: Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes command
description: See DRIVERS-197 for details.

summary: Make shared access to bson_ts more explicit
description:
In the driver, all {{bson_t}} s are wrapped and owned by {{DocumentStorage}} instances, which are used as the underlying storage for {{Document}} structs. 

In many places in the driver, we provide {{libmongoc}} direct access to those {{bson_t}} s: for example, when passing in options, or when providing an empty {{bson_t}} for libmongoc to fill out with the server's reply.

This is problematic because {{Document}} s are designed to be copy-on-write; before any mutations are done on a {{Document}}, the {{Document}} checks whether its underlying {{DocumentStorage}} is uniquely referenced or not. If it's not, the {{Document}} will make a copy and cede its original storage before it makes a mutation.
When we circumvent the {{Document}}'s mutating methods and allow a {{bson_t}} to be directly modified, we perform no such checks. 

If someone else had a conflicting reference to a document where we do a sneaky mutation like this, we could inadvertently modify their document.

It also breaks Swift guarantees about mutability that a {{Document}} struct declared with a {{let}} can still be mutated.

We should consider how to make more explicit the places we pass pointers to libmongoc, and ideally differentiate between when a mutable and a non-mutable pointer is being passed.

One idea for this is to provide a {{mutating}} method on Document taking a closure, similar to {{withUnsafePointer}}, that allows the caller direct write access to the underlying {{Document}}. The {{Document}} method could do the necessary testing for a unique reference to copy storage if it's required before executing the closure.

summary: Allow a ClientSession to be provided to MongoCollection.drop and MongoDatabase.drop
description:
According to the sessions spec:

> All {{MongoDatabase}} methods that talk to the server SHOULD be overloaded to take an explicit session parameter. 

 > All {{MongoCollection}} methods that talk to the server, with the exception of estimatedDocumentCount, SHOULD be overloaded to take an explicit session parameter.

Our drop methods do not allow providing a session.

Currently, both of these methods use mongoc helpers that do not accept sessions. 

We should rewrite these methods to accept {{ClientSession}} parameters and use {{mongoc_collection_write_command_with_opts}} to execute the commands.

summary: Make options properties mutable to allow changing properties on them after creation
description:
All of the properties on our options objects are declared with {{let}}, which means you can't set them after declaring the options object.

We should change them to {{var}} so you can do something like the following:
{code:java}var opts = FindOptions()
opts.batchSize = 10{code}
We should also add empty initializers for each options struct so XCode users see that show up in autocomplete.

summary: MongoClient/Database/Collection ReadPreference getters should not return Optionals
description:
{{MongoClient.readPreference}}, {{MongoDatabase.readPreference}}, and {{MongoCollection.readPreference}} are computed properties which all return a {{ReadPreference?}}, aka an optional {{ReadPreference}}.

However, none of those getters will ever actually return {{nil}} now. They always return a value.

We should update the return type of all these computed properties so that they are non-optional \{{ReadPreference}}s instead.

summary: Use constant-time hash comparison functions
description: See DRIVERS-255 for details.

summary: Do not validate the "startingFrom" field in OP_REPLY.
description: See DRIVERS-233 for details.

summary: endSessions command no longer takes `ids` parameter
description: See DRIVERS-427 for details.

summary: Validate that w and wtimeoutMS are non-negative in WriteConcern initializer
description:
The {{WriteConcern}} spec tests contain a couple of cases checking for invalid values of {{wTimeoutMS}} and {{w}}. (Specifically, it tests negative values.)

Currently we skip these in {{ReadWriteConcernTests.testDocuments}}, because the initializer relies on libmongoc to validate the options, and libmongoc does not consider those to be invalid values.

However, we could just validate ourselves that they are non-negative. 

We should update the {{WriteConcern}} initializer that looks like this: 
{code:java}
public init(journal: Bool? = nil, w: W? = nil, wtimeoutMS: Int64? = nil) throws{code}
 

to throw a {{UserError.invalidArgumentError}} when either {{w}} or {{wtimeoutMS}} is < 0.

summary: All BSONValue types should be Hashable using 4.2+ Hashable API
description:
We should make all of our custom types that conform to {{BSONValue}} (for example {{Document}}, {{RegularExpression}}, {{ObjectID}}...) conform to the [{{Hashable}} protocol|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/hashable]. 

We should then update {{AnyBSONValue}}'s {{Hashable}} implementation to simply hash the underlying {{BSONValue}}.

summary: MongoDatabase.drop and MongoCollection.drop should allow specifying a WriteConcern
description:
A write concern can be provided to the [dropDatabase command|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/dropDatabase/] and the [drop|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/drop/#dbcmd.drop](Collection) command. 

We should
 * add {{DropDatabaseOptions}} containing a {{WriteConcern}} and allow users to optionally provide those options to {{MongoDatabase.drop}}.
 * add {{DropCollectionOptions}} containing a {{WriteConcern}} and allow users to optionally provide those options to {{MongoCollection.drop}}.

summary: AnyBSONValue.init(from decoder: Decoder) does not check all possible BSONValue types
description:
In this method we try to decoding to various possible BSON types and see which one succeeds.

However we are missing Timestamp, BSONUndefined, and DBPointer. 

This is a pretty bad bug and not the first time we've run into an issue because there is no way to exhaustively switch on all BSONValue types. 

Separately from this I have opened another ticket SWIFT-509 suggesting we figure out a way to avoid these bugs in the future.

summary: ReadConcern and WriteConcern should no longer wrap mongoc types
description:
Currently {{ReadConcern}} wraps a {{mongoc_read_concern_t}} and {{WriteConcern}} wraps a {{mongoc_write_concern_t}}.

However, in most places we use {{ReadConcern}}s and {{WriteConcern}}s , we never actually use the mongoc type for anything besides storing properties. We construct {{ReadConcern}} and {{WriteConcern}} documents ourselves which we insert into options documents. 

The only places we actually need the mongoc types are when we set a \{[ReadConcern}} or {{WriteConcern}} on a {{MongoClient}}, {{MongoDatabase}}, or {{MongoCollection}}. 

We should think about no longer wrapping the mongoc types, and just adding e.g. {{withMongocReadConcern}} and {{withMongocWriteConcern}} helpers to use in those couple of places that temporarily constructs the equivalent libmongoc types. 

This would allow us to make these types structs which seems more fitting and would cut down on any performance penalty from interop.

We could also consider doing this for {{ReadPreference}}, though we actually do use the libmongoc types every time for those since they are separate arguments in the read helpers and not part of the options structs.

summary: Support maxTimeMS for createIndexes and dropIndexes command
description: See DRIVERS-374 for details.

summary: Standardize index command options
description: Currently, the options passed to index related commands are labelled {{commandOptions}}. It would be more consistent with our other commands if they were labelled simply {{options}}.

summary: Ensure that docs are updated when public API changes are made
description:
Right now, we have to just remember to regenerate docs when a public facing change is made. It is excessive to do it on every commit, since often changes are just internal.
However, running {{make documentation}} will always generate a diff even when no public changes are made, because every file is updated with a "last updated" date. 

[~may.hoque@10gen.com] suggested that we could determine when changes are needed by taking a diff between the result of {{make documentation}} on master and the result of {{make documentation}} on the PR. There should only be a difference there when changes are actually needed. 

While we might include that as a test run on Travis, we don't want it to run on PRs, as it would force every PR with docs changes to include them in the PR to pass CI. Given the number of files that get modified, this is really annoying for a PR reviewer. So ideally, this would only run on master on Travis, and a broken build would alert the committer they need to make a second "regenerate docs" commit.

We should figure out the best way to incorporate this.

Alternatively, we could only update docs when we tag a release. in that case, we should figure out how to force regenerating docs when we do that.  The downside of that is anything we forgot to document up till then would need to be taken care of at the end when we release which might be annoying; doing it the other way would force us to document things as we write them. _However_, since it would be annoying to have to deal with forgotten documentation when we release, we could try to avoid this by turning on a {{swiftlint}} rule for complaining when a declaration does not have documentation attached to it which can now be done with [{{missing_docs}}|https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/blob/master/Rules.md#missing-docs].

Building from the above idea, a finer grained approach of running an Evergreen-like (think {{Waterfall}}) task on every commit/merge to master via Travis (and not on every PR) may allow us to deal with the issue of forgotten documentation pretty soon after closing a ticket, as opposed to during release.

summary: Ensure MongoClient, MongoCollection and MongoDatabase are thread safe
description: Make sure that these classes are fully thread safe, ensuring any internal state is appropriately locked for multithreaded use. In particular inherited properties such as {{writeConcern}}, {{readPreference}}, etc.

summary: Add APM assertions to change stream spec tests
Modify Change Stream Output Examples
description: See DRIVERS-537 for details

summary: Move listCollections filter to top level
description: In SWIFT-174, we implemented listDatabases with the {{filter}} not in the options but at the top-level. We should update listCollections to do this also.

summary: Make ServerVersion CustomStringConvertible
MongoCursor.next should check for an error before trying to get the next document
description:
Currently we just go ahead and try to call {{mongoc_cursor_next}} even if {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}} has already returned an error. 
 This might lead to overwriting some original error with an error about trying to iterate a failed cursor.

Instead, we should save off any errors returned by {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}}, and then anytime {{next}} is called check if that error is non-nil before trying to iterate again.

summary: Make MongoDatabase and MongoCollection structs
description:
For a reason we have not been able to determine, switching MongoDatabase and MongoCollection to be structs causes a Swift compiler crash in the ClientSessionTests.

I spent some time trying to create a minimal example to repro the crash but did not have any luck. We should maybe timebox spending a bit more time on that.

Otherwise I think we have to just play around with the structure of the tests until they don't cause a crash anymore.

summary: Return Connection to pool as soon as MongoCursor is exhausted
description:
Currently, if a {{MongoCursor}} uses its own {{Connection}} (i.e. not one from a {{ClientSession}}) the {{MongoCursor}} will return the {{Connection}} to the pool in deinit.

However, this could lead to situations where a user has a number of exhausted cursors lying around and exhausts the connection pool.

One change that will be needed to support this is storing the result of {{mongoc_cursor_error_document}}. Otherwise a user will not be able to check for an error after the cursor is exhausted (which they are supposed to do once {{next()}} returns {{nil}}, as {{next()}} can't throw.)

summary: Make BulkWriteOperation consistent with other Operation types
description:
While {{BulkWriteOperation}} does conform to the {{Operation}} protocol, it is very different than the other {{Operation}} types. 
It has various methods to add write models to it, and doesn't actually use the {{Connection}} passed into its {{execute}} method.

We should figure out how to refactor it such that it follows the pattern of other {{Operation}} s. All of the write models should be passed into the operation's initializer, and the execute method should actually use the provided connection.

summary: Figure out a better way to handle encoding IndexModel + IndexOptions
description: Since the options need to be at the same level in the document as the index name and keys, we have to manually encode the model and options separately and then merge them. We should figure out a cleaner way to do this.

summary: Implement index enumeration spec
description:
We should implement the [index enumeration spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/enumerate-indexes.rst] using the same approach we took when implementing the db enumeration spec (SWIFT-174).

Note that this spec is in the draft phase for some reason, though it is quite old and appears to have stabilized enough for us to implement.

summary: Implement collection enumeration spec
description:
We should implement the [collection enumeration spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/enumerate-collections.rst] using the same approach we took when implementing the db enumeration spec (SWIFT-174).

Note that this spec is in the draft phase for some reason, though it is quite old and appears to have stabilized enough for us to implement.

summary: Use Operations when creating and iterating a change stream
description:
{{MongoCursor.next}} uses {{NextOperation}} for executing most of the cursor iteration logic. Since {{ChangeStream.next}} behaves similarly and performs I/O, it should also have its logic in an operation such as {{ChangeStreamNextOperation}}. Another possible option would be to just modify the existing {{NextOperation}} to also process a {{ChangeStream}}.
{{NextOperation}} is a little weird now in that we don't execute it via executor, as its execute method ignores the passed-in connection, and we don't want to wastefully check out and back in a connection we don't use every time a cursor is iterated.

We should also Operation-ize the creation of {{ChangeStream}} s via some {{watch}} operation.

summary: Add special executor logic for operations that hold connections
description:
{{NextOperation}} and {{ChangeStreamNextOperation}} s (added via SWIFT-523) are unique in that they don't need new connections to be executed as their corresponding cursors and change streams hold onto connections.

{{WatchOperation}} is also unique in that it needs to check out a connection and then give it to the change stream. 

We should add special logic to the executor / operation protocol to handle these cases.

summary: wtimeoutMS is truncated if set to a value > Int32.max
description: The {{wtimeoutMS}} field of a write concern is spec-defined to be an {{int64}} (which we accept), but libmongoc only accepts {{int32}} s, so we truncate if necessary in order to pass to libmongoc.

summary: Decimal128.writeToCurrentPosition should pass a non-mutable pointer to bson_iter_overwrite_decimal128
description: As of CDRIVER-3069 this method takes in a const pointer. Once we bump to mongoc 1.15 as our min version we can stop passing in a mutable pointer here.

summary: Consider improvement to typing for bulk write models
description:
The InsertOneModel and ReplaceOneModel structs for {{MongoCollection.bulkWrite()}} depend on the generic CollectionType. For consistency, all bulk write models are declared within the MongoCollection class. This requires either verbose syntax (e.g. {{MongoCollection<Document>.InsertOneModel}}) or type aliases like the following:

{noformat}
private typealias DeleteOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.DeleteOneModel
private typealias DeleteManyModel = MongoCollection<Document>.DeleteManyModel
private typealias InsertOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.InsertOneModel
