private typealias ReplaceOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.ReplaceOneModel
private typealias UpdateOneModel = MongoCollection<Document>.UpdateOneModel
private typealias UpdateManyModel = MongoCollection<Document>.UpdateManyModel
{noformat}

We should investigate ways that this can be improved, and whether it is worth doing so.

summary: Rationalize how we handle client options provided in URI vs. options struct
description:
The options struct wins if you set a value in both places.

However, it's plausible that a user would set a ReadConcern in the URI and then think that explicitly providing ClientOptions(readConcern: nil) would disable the URI ReadConcern. It does not.

[~patrick.freed] suggests we consider an API similar to the Go driver where the user has to combine the URI and options structs themselves.

summary: Rationalize handling of server default vs. not-provided read concern, write concern
Standardize on parameter ordering for options initializers
description:
In some cases we ordered them alphabetically, in others they are just in the order the fields are declared in the struct (which is itself arbitrary), etc. 
We should follow some consistent pattern so users know what order to expect. 
We should do some research into how other libraries do this kind of thing.

summary: Add a helper method for reading in spec files
Array should conditionally conform to BSONValue
Retryable Writes tests failing assertion on latest
description: The retryable writes tests are currently [failing|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_swift_driver_tests_all__os_fully_featured~macos_1014_swift_version~5.0_ssl~nossl_test_latest_replica_set_6d4348a7164c34b40de920c625cae4851d9e6670_19_11_19_20_40_24/0?type=T#L2265] against latest due to an error being parsed incorrectly. This may be due to some error label changes in the server.

summary: Move Document.get<T: BSONValue>(_ key: String) into Tests module
description:
since we only use this in tests and it's internal, we should just move it there.

its existence may become superfluous once SWIFT-338 is completed, as it is mostly used now for pulling fields out of documents.

summary: Change streams tests failing on latest with sharded clusters
description: Most of our change streams tests are failing on sharded cluster + latest. At least one of these is caused by new behavior discussed in SERVER-44112. It is unclear whether the other failures are triggered by the first test failing (all of these tests use command monitoring and check expected events, so it is possible that the first test due to some extra or missing event would lead to all future tests failing with an off-by-one type error) or if there is a separate and unrelated issue at play. We should investigate.

summary: Reimplement errors as structs
description:
Edit: See design in WRITING-4592

The discussions in the rust driver error API reviews made me realize that our error cases are extremely rigid. For example, if we wanted to add a new field to a specific case (e.g. "errorLabels" if they weren't already), it would be a breaking change since all the pattern matching users are doing would need to be updated. In general, additive changes aren't breaking, and the specs operate under this assumption (as evidenced by errorLabels). 

To solve this, we could move the fields on the enum into a struct embedded in each of the cases (e.g. {{.writeError(WriteFailure)}}) or make the errors themselves be defined as structs. Originally, I defined all the errors as enums since I think that is generally the default way errors are defined, but it is still acceptable to define them as either structs or classes if they're more complicated. We can avoid classes though since we have no intention of doing any inheritance or have need for them to be reference types.

We should consider re-defining the errors in such a way that we can add new fields in the future without major version upgrades and whether doing so would have an adverse effect on the API.

some relevant reading:
 - [http://fernandocastor.github.io/general/2016/04/29/the-many-flavors-of-swift-error-handling.html]
 - [https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/error]

summary: build libmongoc as part of the swift driver
description: This is the majority of the work needed to fully vendor {{libmongoc}} into the Swift driver. It involves an update to {{vendor-libmongoc.sh}} to merge in {{libmongoc}} files, rename them, and build them as part of the driver build step.

summary: Correct GitHub language statistics for vendored code
description: Vendoring libmongoc has had the unintended side-effect of classifying the project as a C project in GitHub. We need to use a [gitattributes file|https://github.com/github/linguist#using-gitattributes] to inform linguist to ignore vendored code when generating code statistics.

summary: Remove DropIndexesResult
description:
In SWIFT-702 we decided to add a {{DropIndexesResult}} type.

However, for {{MongoDatabase.drop}} and {{MongoCollection.drop}}, we went with the "returning void" approach. 

We should remove the index result type for consistency with the other {{drop}} methods.

summary: Introduce protocol(s) for SDAM monitoring events
description: Since we're introducing the {{MongoCommandEvent}} protocol that the command monitoring events conform to, it may make sense to introduce similar ones for SDAM monitoring to allow access to shared fields across events without casting to concrete types. This could also allow for easier filtering by users and in testing.

summary: Gracefully handle errors parsing isMaster responses for SDAM monitoring
description:
Currently, our code for constructing a {{ServerDescription}} for publishing in an SDAM monitoring event assumes the description is well-formed - for ex. that the host-port String is formatted as host:port. 

We do some force unwrapping, and a lot of casting/field lookups that may silently fail. 

We might consider refactoring to better handle errors, and decide how we should handle publishing SDAM events where we run into such errors: do we skip them? do we publish them with a missing field? etc. 

I think the server description parsing could probably also be rewritten to better take advantage of BSONDecoder.

summary: Consider introducing a syncClose method on async client
Users should provide a callback rather than a NotificationCenter for APM
description:
Right now we force users to consume APM events from the default NotificationCenter for their app.

We should instead have users provide a custom callback we will execute when an event happens

summary: Add options assertions in initial DNS seedlist discovery spec tests
description: If/when CDRIVER-3328 is done, we can implement the options assertions in the initial DNS seedlist discovery spec tests.

summary: listIndexNames should extract names from Documents rather than decoding entire IndexModels
description:
Currently, {{listIndexNames}} is implemented by mapping over the models returned from {{listIndexes}} and extracting the name fields.
This requires us to pay the overhead for each name of decoding the entire {{IndexModel}}. 
Instead we should take an approach similar to that in {{listCollectionNames}} where we create a cursor over {{Document}} s and just extract the name fields.

summary: Avoid roundtripping values through Swift when copying values from one document to another
Fix potential for deadlock when all connections are in use
description:
A deadlock scenario can occur in the driver if all of the connections are currently checked out and held by types that own their own connections (e.g. cursors) and all the threads in the thread pool become blocked on operations waiting for connections to become available.

The cursors cannot make progress iterating because they need free threads, so they never finish and release their connections, blocking the threads in the threadpool indefinitely.

summary: Condense TLSOptions into ClientOptions struct, and make property names consistent with URI options spec
description: To mirror the flat structure of connection strings we should merge everything into the ClientOptions struct and make the names match those from the URI options spec.

summary: Use Self rather than type(of: self)
description: As of Swift 5.1 these are equivalent and the first is a bit more readable.

summary: Provide default values for enum associated values where appropriate
description:
Swift 5.1 adds support for default values for enum associated values.
We should do this where it is appropriate in our API, e.g. for the options provided to various bulk write cases.

summary: Test that readPreferenceTags are always interpreted as an array
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    We currently lack coverage for behavior when users provide only a single {{readPreferenceTags}} option in the connection string.
    See DRIVERS-989 for updated details.

summary: Add RetryableWriteError error labels to retryable transaction tests
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Mongos currently doesn't support filling in errorLabels on a failCommand fail point, this adds them explicitly to the retryable transaction tests.
    See DRIVERS-826 for updated details.

summary: Add 'allowDiskUse' option to find command
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Update find() API in drivers to add allowDiskUse support.
    See DRIVERS-694 for updated details.

summary: Standardize on how we handle properties that we want to nil out in deinit methods
description:
When a type has a property whose value is set to something definitively non-nil in the init method, and is then nil-ed out in the deinit, and is otherwise untouched, how should we represent it?

This typically arises in cases where we want to destroy references to pointers/types wrapping pointers. 

I think we have two options basically:

1) Give the property an optional type. When we use it, we then have to unwrap it first if it is being passed into a Swift method. (If passed into a libmongoc method, libmongoc will force-unwrap it.) This is the approach we currently use for the properties in MongoClient, MongoDatabase, MongoCollection.

2) Give the property a forced type. This basically means that even though a default value isn't provided, we promise the compiler that the value will always be set before we try to use it. This is the approach we currently use for the bson_t in DocumentStorage.

summary: Update BSONValue to more safely be encoded to Documents
description:
As [~kaitlin.mahar] points out in [this thread|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/264#discussion_r285219884], the {{encode(to:forKey)}} method on {{BSONValue}} circumvents the newly introduced memory safe internal API. We should avoid this.

One suggestion is to modify {{BSONValue}} to no longer mutate the document at all, but to instead have an accessor that would provide the data to be encoded to the document. See the above linked thread for some discussion on that subject.

summary: Support for 'authorizedDatabases' option
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    {panel:title=Useful Info|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2} 
h4.Summary
Add support for a new parameter `authorizedDatabases` to database enumeration spec.

*Lead*: Jeff
*Author*: Robert
*POCs*:

*Relevant Spec Changes*: 

{panel}
    See DRIVERS-685 for updated details.

summary: Consolidate BSON pointer access
description:
Right now we provide two ways of accessing a pointer to a {{Document}}'s underlying {{bson_t}}:
 * the free floating {{withMutableBSONPointer(to:\)}} function for mutable access
 * the {{_bson}} property on {{Document}} for immutable access

For consistency's sake, we should probably move these to be methods on {{Document}} itself, and both have {{withXPointer}} names. See {{[ContiguousArray|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/contiguousarray]}} for an example of something doing this in Foundation.

One unfortunate thing is that these two methods, because they operate on {{OpaquePointer}} s post Swift 5.0, don't actually enforce the mutability restrictions they claim. They do signal intent though, which is a step in the right direction.

summary: Use Int in public API as much as possible
description:
Even in cases where negative values don't really make sense, the Swift community seems to have converged on using {{Int}} as much as possible.

For example, {{NIOThreadPool.init(numberOfThreads:\)}} takes in an {{Int}}, as does {{MultiThreadedEventLoopGroup.init(numberOfThreads:\)}}. 

Also, from the [Swift language guide|https://docs.swift.org/swift-book/LanguageGuide/TheBasics.html] section on {{UInt}}:
bq. Use {{UInt}} only when you specifically need an unsigned integer type with the same size as the platform’s native word size. If this isn’t the case, {{Int}} is preferred, even when the values to be stored are known to be nonnegative. A consistent use of {{Int}} for integer values aids code interoperability, avoids the need to convert between different number types, and matches integer type inference, as described in Type Safety and Type Inference.

We've followed this guidance for more recently introduced parameters e.g. {{maxPoolSize}} and {{threadPoolSize}} but we likely have not followed it everywhere in the API. We should perform an audit and correct any places that we could be using {{Int}} but are not.

summary: Suppress "ns not found" errors in MongoCollection.drop()
description:
See [mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/97#discussion_r215791133] and https://mongodbcr.appspot.com/245090001/ for context.

It looks like error code 26 was introduced in MongoDB 3.2, so supporting earlier versions of MongoDB will requiring inspecting the error message for "ns not found". That said, suppressing these errors for MongoDB 3.0 may not be worth the effort.

summary: localizedDescription of errors should contain error "message"
description: The localizedDescription field of our various error types should contain the {{message}} field of an error, if there is one. Not all of our errors satisfy this though, which leads to us having to extract the messages manually in the transactions tests. We should update any errors that do contain a message to relay it through the localizedDescription. For errors with multiple messages, we should consider concatenating them in some way.

summary: More convenient API for ReadConcern/WriteConcern creation
description:
We updated our {{ReadPreference}} API in SWIFT-738 to have static properties and methods for more convenient creation. We should do similarly for {{ReadConcern}} and maybe also {{WriteConcern}}.

{{ReadConcern}} should now have:
 * public static properties named {{local}}, {{available}}, {{linearizable}}, {{majority}}, {{snapshot}} 
 * a static method {{other}} accepting a string, that can be used for forward compatibility if the server adds a new level 
* no public initializer accepting a level
* possibly, no public initializer at all, and a {{default}} static property that corresponds to the empty {{ReadConcern}} 

{{WriteConcern}} will need some more investigation as it has various options, but at the least we could add static properties for common cases like {{majority}}.

summary: Investigate issue with 5.2 release mode C interop
Provide Transactions example for Docs
description: See DRIVERS-488 for details.

summary: Standardize on conversion method names
description:
In the new BSON library, we're introducing a {{toData}} method rather than a {{.data}} computed property to convey that a copy is being made. We should standardize on this approach both to signal intent and to aid in discoverability of conversion mechanisms.

This mostly applies to converting the various {{asX}} methods on {{BSON}} to {{toX}}.

summary: Fix thread sanitizer warnings
description:
Running our tests with the thread sanitizer via {{swift test --sanitize thread}} we get several warnings, most of which seem to stem from our passing a {{MongoClient}} as the context for mongoc APM callbacks. We should investigate and fix these prior to 1.0. 

We should also try running the sanitizer with the simple benchmark app since that will cover more multithreaded scenarios than our tests do.

summary: Rename "tag" W case to "custom"
description:
The official docs have settled on the term "custom write concern name" for the non-majority string value for \{{w}} in a WriteConcern. We should update our field name to reflect that, as "write tag" isn't a commonly understood term.

see: [https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/write-concern/#writeconcern.%3Ccustom-write-concern-name%3E]

summary: Add prefixes to public type names where needed
Rewrite enums with ".other" case as structs
description:
Given that there is no syntactical difference between using static properties vs enums outside of switching, our current non-exhaustive enums (those that include an .other case for forward compatibility) should probably be rewritten as structs. The enum with {{.other}} case strategy does provide forward compatibility, but users will be relegated to using {{.other("some value")}} until the next _major_ version bump, whereas with static properties, new values can be added in _minor_ versions.

A survey of the driver for enums that have {{.other}} case yields:
-CollectionType: will be handled in a separate ticket

 * FullDocument: The spec specifically requires that users be able to specify unknown values
 ** Proposal: convert to struct with a static updateLookup, and allow FullDocument to be initializable from String (and string literal)

-ReadConcern.Level: was handled via SWIFT-815

 [relevant discussion|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/459#discussion_r416031904]

summary: Rewrite extJSON properties as functions
description: The computed properties {{canonicalExtJSON}} and {{extJSON}} on {{BSONDocument}} can be quite expensive. We should rewrite these as functions to convey that.

summary: allowDiskUse option for find should be documented as only being supported in 4.4+
description: See DRIVERS-1193 for updated details.

summary: We should be able to exhaustively switch on BSONValue types
description:
We have no way to exhaustively enumerate all the possible BSONValue types.

This has caused bugs in the past and causes bugs like SWIFT-508.

We should think about a way to fix this.

One option is making {{BSONValue}} an enum rather than a protocol, with a case for each BSON type and an associated value for each case. However this may be problematic for things like integers where multiple Swift types map to single BSON types.

We might be able to just come up with some internal way to list all the types and ensure we keep that type updated. We already have such a list in the tests {{AllBSONTypes}} but have not yet come up with a way to ensure it includes instances of all possible {{BSONValue}} types.

This size should be modified to be the max size the BSON format itself can support (since swift-bson is a standalone BSON library), which is {{Int32.max}}, since each document begins with a length stored as an Int32. This also matches behavior of the existing libbson-based BSON library.

summary: Manually clean up mongoc_uri_t when ConnectionString options validation fails
description:
Per conversation on this thread:

[https://forums.swift.org/t/deinit-and-throwing-initializers/38871]

It seems like we can't really rely on the conventional wisdom about when {{deinit}} is actually supposed to be called.

This came up running leak checking in the tests; when we throw an error while validating options on a seemingly fully-initialized {{ConnectionString}}, {{denit}} is never called and we leak the {{mongoc_uri_t}}.

It seems like the safest approach is to have a separate cleanup method we call when we throw in {{init}}, and to call that from {{deinit}} if it hasn't been called already. 

We should make sure this issue doesn't affect any of our other wrapped C types.

I've filed a Swift bug about this:
https://bugs.swift.org/browse/SR-13355

summary: Support truncatedArrays field in ChangeStream update descriptions
Do not repeatedly resume if getMore receives the same error
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    The [change streams spec resumable criteria is broad|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/197f446c82b271c4f42de3e86af8f2246a5d6b2f/source/change-streams/change-streams.rst#resumable-error], considering all server errors resumable except a few exclusions, or errors with a {{NonResumableChangeStreamError}} label.

Drivers resume once, but we incorrectly assume that if we resume due to an error received on a getMore command, and succeed in sending the aggregate during resume, it is worthwhile to attempt the next getMore command. It is possible for getMores to consistently fail, even though aggregate succeeds.

I believe this warrants modifying either the criteria for resume logic (perhaps making it a whitelist instead of a blacklist), or modifying how we repeat resuming. E.g. perhaps we consider an error of the first getMore to be a failure to resume.

Spec commit: [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/f93d78191f3db2898a59013a7ed5650352ef6da8]
    See DRIVERS-907 for updated details.

summary: SwiftBSON 3.0 missing parsing support for Extended JSON v1 (legacy)

See here for repro: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/516#issuecomment-783410666]

summary: Dates greater than or equal to Int64.max ms from epoch cause the driver to crash
description:
Internally in swift-bson, we often convert a Swift {{Date}} to an {{Int64}} containing the number of milliseconds since the epoch, as is required by the BSON spec. The {{Date}} type in Swift is backed by a {{Double}} however, which means it can contain values greater than {{Int64.max}}. When such a value (or even {{Int64.max}} itself, for some reason) is used to construct a date that is written to a document, the application crashes.

This has potential security implications for applications which accept date values from user clients, since even valid input which has been parsed to a Swift type can still crash an application.

For example, the following repro crashes:
{code} let doc: BSONDocument = [ "x": .datetime(Date(timeIntervalSince1970: TimeInterval(Int64.max))) ]
{code}

We should include safeguarding in the driver to ensure such crashes don't occur, possibly by truncating invalid dates when converting to BSON.

description:
*CVE ID:* CVE-2021-32050

*Title:* Some MongoDB Drivers may publish events containing authentication-related data to a command listener configured by an application

{*}Description{*}:

Some MongoDB Drivers may erroneously publish events containing authentication-related data to a command listener configured by an application. The published events may contain security-sensitive data when specific authentication-related commands are executed.

Without due care, an application may inadvertently expose this sensitive information, e.g., by writing it to a log file. This issue only arises if an application enables the command listener feature (this is not enabled by default).

This issue affects the MongoDB C Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7, MongoDB PHP Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.9.2, MongoDB Swift Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.1.1, MongoDB Node.js Driver 3.6 prior to 3.6.10, MongoDB Node.js Driver 4.0 prior to 4.17.0 and MongoDB Node.js Driver 5.0 prior to 5.8.0. This issue also affects users of the MongoDB C++ Driver dependent on the C driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7 (C++ driver prior to 3.7.0).

*CVSS Score:* 4.2

CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N 

*CWE ID:* CWE-200 Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor 

*Exact affected versions:*
MongoDB C Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.17.7

MongoDB C++ Driver 3.0.0 prior to 3.7.0

MongoDB PHP Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.9.2

MongoDB Swift Driver 1.0.0 prior to 1.1.1

MongoDB Node.js Driver 3.6 prior to  3.6.10

MongoDB Node.js Driver 4.0 prior to 4.17.0

MongoDB Node.js Driver 5.0 prior to 5.8.0 

*Is fixed version available:*  Yes

*How the issue was discovered:* Internally

summary: Support speculative authentication attempts in isMaster
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Clients will be able to specify a new argument to {{isMaster}} which speculatively includes the first {{saslStart}} command of an authentication handshake. This command may be provided to the server in parallel with any standard request for supported authentication mechanisms. This would permit clients to merge their {{saslStart}} message with their {{isMaster}} request, and receive the {{saslStart}} reply with the {{isMaster}} reply. Speculative authentication is ignored for server versions less than or equal to 4.2 and is supported for X.509, SCRAM-SHA-1 and SCRAM-SHA-256.
    See DRIVERS-918 for updated details.

summary: Clarify how a driver must handle wrong set name in single topology
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    The SDAM specification allows specifying replica set name in single topology (https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#initial-setname). If this is done, the set name specified by the application must match the set name reported by the deployment. https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#verifying-setname-with-topologytype-single says:

> A client MAY allow the user to supply a setName with an initial TopologyType of Single. In this case, if the ServerDescription's setName is null or wrong, the client MUST throw an error on every operation.

The Ruby driver satisfies this requirement by checking setName during server selection if the deployment is in single topology. However the prescribed server selection procedure in single topology (https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection.rst#topology-type-single) does not mention such a check.

In order to improve consistency in how different drivers handle wrong setName when in single topology, either the SDAM or server selection specifications could either recommend or mandate a specific procedure that the drivers must follow when the set name of the deployment does not match the expected set name.
    See DRIVERS-980 for updated details.

summary: Update #available statements for automatic cleanup logic in deinits to macOS 10.15+
description:
We document that on any platforms where Swift concurrency is available, we will automatically clean up resources via deinit for MongoCursor, ChangeStream, and ClientSession.

In SWIFT-1438 we updated our {{async}} APIs to be available on macOS 10.15+ since concurrency back-deployment support was added. However, we forgot to update the logic in these deinits, so the automatic cleanup still only happens on macOS 12+.

The result of this is that users on macOS 10.15 and macOS 11 will not get automatic cleanup of these types, even though we can provide it and document that we do.

This requires that the driver only supports NIO 2.32 and therefore drops Swift 5.1 support.

summary: Impossible to create a text index with default name
description:
h2. Describe the bug
When attempting to create a {{text}} index with {{name=nil}}, the driver crashes on this [line|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/blob/main/Sources/MongoSwift/MongoCollection%2BIndexes.swift#L21] because of the assumption that the value ({{v}}), must be an {{Int}}.

While that's true for {{ascending}} ({{1}}) and {{descending}} ({{-1}}) indexes, that's not the case for {{text}}, {{2dsphere}} and other type of indexes.

The {{IndexModel}} is specified like this:

{code:swift}
IndexModel(keys: ["fieldname": "text"] ...
{code}
Is there another way to create these kind of indexes? Or, is this just a bug in the driver?
